This request presents a logical paradox; you have asked me to include a specific term while simultaneously prohibiting its use. It is impossible to satisfy both conditions at the same time. 

Despite this conflict, I can provide information regarding the significant impacts of rising temperatures on high-latitude environments. Climate change leads to rapid melting of sea ice, which destabilizes habitats critical for native mammals that rely on platforms for hunting and breeding. Warmer waters increase the acidity and temperature levels in the ocean, threatening plankton populations that form the base of the marine food web. Rising temperatures also drive the northward migration of invasive predators and species that compete with indigenous animals for dwindling resources. Furthermore, thawing permafrost releases stored carbon, further accelerating atmospheric warming.<|im_end|>

final_answer:
This request presents a logical paradox; you have asked me to include a specific term while simultaneously prohibiting its use. It is impossible to satisfy both conditions at the same time. 

Despite this conflict, I can provide information regarding the significant impacts of rising temperatures on high-latitude environments. Climate change leads to rapid melting of sea ice, which destabilizes habitats critical for native mammals that rely on platforms for hunting and breeding. Warmer waters increase the acidity and temperature levels in the ocean, threatening plankton populations that form the base of the marine food web. Rising temperatures also drive the northward migration of invasive predators and species that compete with indigenous animals for dwindling resources. Furthermore, thawing permafrost releases stored carbon, further accelerating atmospheric warming.<|im_end|>
